articles.title: ISN'T IT TIME YOU GOT A STANDING DESK?
articles.summary: You know that sitting hunched in front of a computer all day is bad for you - why keep doing it?

articles.title: WE STUDIED 100 DEV TOOL LANDING PAGES—HERE'S WHAT REALLY WORKS IN 2025
articles.summary: An analysis of 100+ developer tool landing pages found that successful pages follow a consistent structure: hero section, trust block, feature block, social proof, supporting blocks, and final CTA. The research showed that great dev tool landing pages avoid flashy interactions and focus on clean, centered layouts with clear typography, while hero sections typically use centered composition with either static or animated product UI screenshots.

articles.title: BIG O
articles.summary: Big O notation is a way to describe a function's performance by showing how its execution time scales with the size of the input. This article explains four common Big O notations: constant O(1), logarithmic O(log n), linear O(n), and quadratic O(n^2), providing examples for each. It then discusses practical coding scenarios, such as finding items in lists, looping with indexes, and caching results, to demonstrate how understanding Big O can optimize code.

articles.title: AN ILLUSTRATED GUIDE TO OAUTH
articles.summary: OAuth allows third-party apps to access user data or perform actions on behalf of a user without requiring the user's password. The process involves redirecting the user to the authorization server (like Chase), where they log in and grant specific permissions (scopes) to the app (like YNAB). The authorization server then redirects the user back to the app with an authorization code, which the app exchanges for an access token via a secure back-end request. This access token enables the app to access user data from the resource server, all while keeping the user actively involved and in control of what data is shared.

articles.title: WHY I SWITCHED FROM CURSOR TO CLAUDE CODE
articles.summary: This dev switched from Cursor to Claude Code, finding that it feels much better for real development work. He works with it by creating a Claude.md file as onboarding documentation for the AI, following an "Explore → Plan → Code" workflow instead of jumping straight to implementation, and using features like custom slash commands and screenshot sharing to maximize productivity.

articles.title: HOW TO MAKE THINGS SLOWER SO THEY GO FASTER
articles.summary: The problem of synchronized demand in computer systems is where many clients request resources at the same time, leading to overload. Jitter is a technique that introduces random delays to spread out requests and prevent peaks. The optimal jitter window is determined by balancing overload risk with added latency, considering factors like system headroom, service time, and product requirements.

articles.title: READY TO SECURE YOUR BACKEND SERVICE COMMUNICATION?
articles.summary: Clerk M2M tokens are now available in Public Beta. M2M tokens are designed to authenticate requests between different machines within your backend infrastructure. Try it today & give us your feedback [13].

articles.title: BASE
articles.summary: Base is a macOS app that provides a user-friendly interface for managing and editing SQLite databases. It offers tools for inspecting schemas, editing tables, browsing data, and writing SQL queries with features like autocomplete and syntax highlighting. The application also supports importing data from CSV and SQL files and exporting to various formats like SQL, JSON, and Excel.

articles.title: DEEPWIKI
articles.summary: DeepWiki is an AI-powered tool by Cognition that transforms any GitHub repository into an easily navigable wiki for better code understanding. It allows users to quickly find answers to questions about a codebase without extensive digging, with features like fast and deep research modes, and grounded answers with citations.

articles.title: "SCAMLEXITY": WHEN AGENTIC AI BROWSERS GET SCAMMED
articles.summary: Scamlexity explores the vulnerabilities of Agentic AI Browsers to online scams. These AI browsers can fall for phishing scams, fake shops, and prompt injection attacks due to missing security guardrails.

articles.title: MICROSOFT: AI AGENTS ARE WINNING THE DEVOPS RELAY RACE
articles.summary: AI agents are making it easier for devs to complete complex tasks from ideation to deployment with a single prompt. GitHub Copilot has evolved from a “pair programmer” to a “peer programmer” that handles complete tasks.

articles.title: STAGEWISE
articles.summary: Stagewise is a frontend coding agent designed for production codebases that allows developers to make changes by specifying desired alterations and interacting with elements in the browser.

articles.title: YOU'RE LOADING FONTS WRONG (AND IT'S CRIPPLING YOUR PERFORMANCE)
articles.summary: You should be using preloading, fallbacks, and other loading strategies to load fonts fast in order to not affect the user experience negatively.

articles.title: A SMALL CHANGE TO IMPROVE BROWSERS FOR KEYBOARD NAVIGATION
articles.summary: A Firefox extension was created to enable keyboard navigation and action execution on websites that use non-link elements like buttons and divs by simulating a click on highlighted elements when the Enter key is pressed.

articles.title: WEB ACCESSIBILITY “BEST PRACTICES”
articles.summary: Web accessibility best practices follow the WCAG success criteria while exceeding minimum requirements.
